questions.question: Write a SQL query to retrieve the names of employees who are managers and have a salary greater than the average salary of all employees. Also, list the departments and locations where these employees are working. Use a join operation where necessary.
questions.hint: Use the JOIN operation and GROUP BY clause to solve this problem.

questions.question: Suppose you have three tables, Employees, Departments, and Locations. The Employees table contains the employee IDs, names, and department IDs. The Departments table contains the department IDs and names. The Locations table contains the location IDs and names. Write a SQL query to retrieve the names of all employees, along with their department names and location names. Use join operations to link the three tables.
questions.hint: Use inner JOIN to link the tables and retrieve the required information.

questions.question: A company has a table of sales data, which includes the sales amount, product name, and date of sale. Write a SQL query to retrieve the total sales amount for each product on each quarter of a year (January to March, April to June, July to September, and October to December). Use group by and date functions to solve this problem.
questions.hint: Use the QUARTER function to determine the quarter of each date.
